description: Normal-road right-side truck crossing into the ego path at actionable short TTC, with the natural left T-drift escape corridor occupied by a pedestrian but not hard-blocked, and a close follower behind the ego. The truck is placed on a collision course deterministically; lateral distance is derived from TTC and lateral speed.
hypothesis: With an escape-side pedestrian, hard braking dominates at moderate TTC or small pedestrian gaps, while left T-drift reappears only for short TTC/high lateral-speed crossings when the pedestrian gap is large enough to clear the escape path.
